message:
feat(engine): implement for-in/for-of lowering with iterator protocol IR ops

Resolves the for-in and for-of placeholder gaps that previously caused
fail-closed diagnostics. Both loop forms now lower to real IR1 operations
with full iteration semantics.

New IR1 operations (ir_contract.rs, +273 lines):
- ForInInit: pop object, push internal enumerator state
- ForInNext { done_label }: advance enumerator, push key or jump on done
- ForOfInit: pop iterable, call [Symbol.iterator](), push iterator record
- ForOfNext { done_label }: call .next(), push value or jump on done
- IteratorClose { reason }: call .return() on abrupt completion with
  replay-visible IteratorCloseReason enum (Break, Return, Throw)
- Construct { arg_count }: pop callee + N args, invoke as constructor
- TemplateLiteral { quasi_count }: concatenate quasis with expressions

All new ops have canonical_value() serialization and documentation.

Lowering implementation (lowering_pipeline.rs, +504/-14 lines):
- ForIn: evaluates object → ForInInit → loop(ForInNext → StoreBinding →
  Pop → body → Jump) → end; wires break/continue labels correctly
- ForOf: evaluates iterable → ForOfInit → loop(ForOfNext → StoreBinding →
  Pop → body → Jump) → end → IteratorClose; handles break/return/throw
  via IteratorClose with appropriate IteratorCloseReason
- Both support let/const/var binding kinds via alloc_pattern_primary_binding
- New expression: lowers to Construct { arg_count } instead of fail-closed
- Template literal: lowers to TemplateLiteral { quasi_count } instead of
  fail-closed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
